meta.reasoning: As leader for Quest 1 (size 2), proposing myself (s1) and s2, both confirmed Good players. This gives us the best chance of a successful quest.

I am Good and I'm on the proposed team. Approving is low-risk: if s1 is Evil and sabotages Quest 1, s1 is immediately exposed (I'm the only other member and I know I'll play success). If s1 is Good, we get an early win. Either outcome is informative.

Team [s1, s2] is all-Good so this quest will succeed regardless. Even if I reject, s4 (Good) will likely approve giving majority. Rejecting here gains nothing and raises suspicion. Better to approve early, build trust, and position myself for future proposals where I can get on the team or steer selection toward s5.
meta.strategic_goal: Maintain cover, build trust with Good players, preserve influence for future quest proposals
